name: Broward Healthcare
description_short: Broward Healthcare Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Fort Lauderdale, FL offering savings accounts, loans, and digital banking services to healthcare workers and their families.
description_long:
Broward Healthcare Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union serving the Fort Lauderdale, Florida community, specifically designed to enhance the financial well-being of healthcare professionals and eligible members. According to NCUA data cited on their website, the institution maintains substantial member deposits across multiple account types, including 9,582 active share savings accounts and 656 money market accounts totaling nearly $17 million.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including high-yield checking and savings accounts, flexible auto loans and mortgage options, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), and tax-advantaged retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH). Their digital services include mobile banking, online bill pay, person-to-person payments, e-statements, and online loan payments. Members can also access expert investment and retirement planning services, dedicated wealth management, and exclusive member discounts.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Broward Healthcare FCU emphasizes member benefits including lower fees and competitive rates. Their account structure shows active product uptake: 490 share certificates, 134 IRA/KEOGH accounts, and robust checking/savings offerings. The institution provides both personal and business financial solutions with an accessible membership application process and online loan applications.

A key limitation is geographic specificity—this credit union primarily serves the Fort Lauderdale, Florida area and members must meet specific eligibility requirements (typically healthcare industry employment or family relationships). The website content is dated April 2026 and lacks current interest rate details, fee schedules, and specific loan terms needed for comprehensive comparison. No information is provided about minimum balance requirements, membership eligibility criteria beyond healthcare affiliation, or branch network size.
services: Share Savings Accounts (Regular savings with competitive rates)
Money Market Accounts (higher-yield with flexible access)
Mobile Banking App (account management on smartphones)
Online Bill Pay (schedule one-time or recurring payments)
Investment and Retirement Planning (expert advisory services)
Wealth Management and Dedicated Financial Planning
pros: Credit union structure typically offers lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
NCUA data shows strong member deposits: 9,582 active savings accounts and $16.9M in money market accounts
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, bill pay, P2P payments, and e-statements
Diverse savings products including high-yield checking, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs
Expert investment and retirement planning services available to members
Flexible auto loans and mortgage options with online application process
Exclusive member benefits and discounts program
cons: Geographic limitation—primarily serves Fort Lauderdale, FL area with no mention of expanded branch network
Website does not disclose membership eligibility criteria, minimum balance requirements, or current interest rates
Limited information on auto loan and mortgage terms, rates, or competitive advantages
No detail on maximum loan amounts, approval timelines, or specific APR ranges offered
Lacking transparency on fee structure for services like bill pay, wire transfers, or account maintenance
best_for: Healthcare workers and their families in the Fort Lauderdale area seeking member-owned financial services
Individuals wanting personalized retirement and investment planning with credit union member benefits
Savers seeking competitive rates on CDs, money market accounts, and tax-advantaged retirement accounts
Local borrowers needing flexible auto loans or mortgages with lower fees than traditional banks
diagnosis: Broward Healthcare Federal Credit Union is best suited for healthcare industry employees and their families in the Fort Lauderdale area seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive rates and personalized financial planning. The primary caveat is geographic limitation and lack of transparent current rates/fees on the website—interested members should contact the institution directly for specific APRs, minimum balances, and eligibility confirmation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
